notes: JUDGEMENT action cards. The ensemble verbs at the parish cemetery on All Souls' Eve: READ NAME (the central act), LIGHT CANDLE (the supportive act), LEAVE FLOWER (a small offering at a vault), RING LAMP (signal between the rows — one of the wrought-iron lamps has a brass bell-pull), WALK ROW (the procession).

cards.title: READ NAME
cards.flavor: At the lectern or at a vault. Stand. Open the missal. Read one name aloud. Say the dates if you have them. The ensemble keeps reading after you. The chain is the chain.

cards.title: LIGHT CANDLE
cards.flavor: Pick a vault. Set the small white candle on the lintel. Strike the match. Light the wick. Cup the flame against the wind until the wick takes.

effects.text: Candle on the lintel. The wick caught on the second match. The flame is small.

cards.title: LEAVE FLOWER
cards.flavor: A single flower from the chapel basket at the gate. Marigolds tonight — they hold the All Souls' colour. Set it at the foot of a vault.

effects.text: A marigold at the base. The wind moves the petals once. The petals stay.

cards.title: RING LAMP
cards.flavor: The central spine's third lamp has a brass bell at its base. Pull the cord once. The bell carries to the W and E rows. The bell means a name is about to be read.

effects.text: Bell once. The W row's reader pauses. The E row's reader looks up. The space for your reading is made.

cards.title: WALK ROW
cards.flavor: End to end. Read each vault's name in your head as you pass. Do not read them aloud; that is for the lectern and for the chosen names. The walking is the body's reading.

effects.text: Row walked. The names register without your voice. The walking is the witness.
